title: Chapter Fifty-Four: The Sword That Sings
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

Yeon-shin looked down at Hwangbo Myeong-rin's corpse.

The blood had stopped spreading. In death, her face had lost its arrogance. What remained was the face of a young woman who had believed herself untouchable until the very last instant.

He looked away.

"This one is not even resisting," Cheong Myeong said, shrugging, and kicked the back of Lazy Flame Dragon's knee.

The young master fell with a thud. He looked up at Yeon-shin and grinned.

"Things have gone wrong. If you came knowing everything, it is extermination. A blue-ranked warrior? I could not even imagine your true identity."

He seemed unconcerned that his half-sister's head lay ten paces away.

*Whether it is the contempt he suffered as an illegitimate son, or simply madness from the Solar Divine Meridian's pain -- I cannot tell. Perhaps both.*

"What was that sword technique?" Lazy Flame Dragon's eyes burned with something that had nothing to do with grief. "A strike I have never seen. What internal energy technique did you learn? Tell me."

"Your sister just died by my sword."

"Those dull ones are none of my concern. They lived in a different world. With the family about to fall, there is no need to mind the elders' feelings." His shadowed eyes created a smile. "Lightning Flash. I want to know all your martial arts."

*Completely mad. Martial arts and pain -- he has gone insane from both.*

Yeon-shin ignored him. He stroked the hilts of the Desolate Sword and Northern Light in turn, thinking.

*The Hwangbo family guard unit was just the preliminary battle. This is a mission to strike one of the Eight Great Families. Extermination will not be achieved through short-term decisive battle. They have branch families and branch offices. An elder council. Elite masters.*

*Good. Strong enemies equipped with various martial arts. Enough to grow from.*

---

"Your efforts were great."

"Thanks to you, we can accomplish a long-standing mission."

The seniors ruffled the heads of all three young warriors. Shin So-bin made a face of disgust. Yeon-shin asked Cheong Myeong and Baek Mi-ryeo what had happened since the Gathering.

"We were terrified when we heard about the thunderclap bombs," Cheong Myeong said, grinning despite the words. "Who knew they would use them like that? We ran here with lightness techniques until our feet were drenched."

"At the blue rank, one should be able to deflect explosive bombs," Baek Mi-ryeo said. She demonstrated a palm technique -- a gentle circle drawn in the air. "This is the Harmony Realm technique. Different from the Force Realm, which releases true energy by brute strength. Softness can control hardness. If you surround your sword or fist with an internal energy barrier, you can send thunderclap bombs back. I will show you as many times as you need."

"I already thought of that."

"Oh?"

"I am curious about several applications, but I believe I understand the principles. I would like you to check my work."

Baek Mi-ryeo stared at him.

Around them, the dantians of surviving Hwangbo warriors were being destroyed. Groans and retching. No one paid them attention. They were men who had surrendered instead of fighting alongside their comrades. The gazes upon them held no kindness.

Yeon-shin watched for a moment, then turned away.

---

The disposal of the Hwangbo heirs became the final question.

"I believe killing them is right," Cheong Myeong said, smiling faintly, without hesitation before three Squad Leaders. "Future trouble. They have seen the martial arts of three Desolate Sect military forces. If we take them prisoner and they escape, counter techniques might emerge."

"There is a point," the Azure Sky Squad Leader said.

"Radiant Demon Wing!" Lazy Flame Dragon raised his head and shouted, his glistening eyes fixed on Yeon-shin alone. "I want to join! I will serve with all my might! I will grovel like a dog -- just let me stay by the Lightning Flash's side!"

"What nonsense," Hyeon Won-chang muttered.

Formless energy burst from the Annihilation Team Leader's index finger. Lazy Flame Dragon's upper body shuddered as if struck by a typhoon.

"Seven Steps Life-Losing Acupoint Sealing," Hyeon Won-chang whispered. "The Annihilation Team Leader's unique martial art."

"Solar Divine Meridian," the Annihilation Team Leader said, his voice flat. "Your true energy carries scorching yang power. My original true energy carries wood energy. Wood generates fire. If you raise your energy, it will only strengthen the seal. Try to unseal it carelessly, and your body's channels twist. You die before taking seven steps. In your case -- you burn."

Lazy Flame Dragon grinned. "So you are saying do not try. I have heard well of the Seven Steps technique."

"You know well. Now be quiet."

When Lazy Flame Dragon raised his head again, the bloodshot whites of his eyes did not look human.

"You turtle-boned skeleton! Burn to death? I am sick of heat! It is just a minor meridian, you--"

Yeon-shin kicked the back of his skull. The man's forehead struck the ground and he went still.

*Better unconscious than dead from a Squad Leader's reflex.*

"Why are you not destroying this one's dantian?" he asked, looking between the three Squad Leaders.

Ma Jin placed a blood-soaked hand on his shoulder. "The Sect Lord commanded it. She said to bring him alive if he is living. His inner power must be kept intact. The Solar Divine Meridian is rare in the world. Perhaps she has something to study."

A strange intuition flared in Yeon-shin's upper dantian. The Sect Lord's order was connected to him.

*Then it must be good.*

SWISH!

The second young master's head fell. The Azure Sky Squad Leader's blade was already returning to its scabbard.

"We cannot let more than one direct line survive," the Annihilation Team Leader said.

Yeon-shin stared at the severed head. The arrogant youth who had picked a fight on Huizhou's main road, lying with eyes wide open and mouth agape. Dignity gone. Life gone.

*Do most martial artists' lives end so futilely?*

*To correct the disorder of people's lives, Desolate Sect follows the martial world's own brutal ways. Paradox.*

He hoped there would not be many missions that conflicted with his heart.

---

The Annihilation Team Leader gathered all three forces on the dry plain as the noon sun climbed.

"We strike the branch families first. A martial arts family's power is not just martial might -- it is connections. We must preemptively annihilate their contacts before other sects can intervene. The main force enters the Hwangbo main house after that."

"Nine branch families," he continued. "One in each county of Jinan. We distribute the blue ranks."

Yeon-shin read the room. Concern, interest, displeasure -- the faces of sixty warriors from three squads.

*What I am about to do will be seen as an attempt to monopolise merit. Which is exactly what it is.*

He raised his hand.

"I wish to volunteer for a solo mission."

Silence.

The Annihilation Team Leader stared. "You want a solo mission?"

"To hone the wide-area martial art I recently acquired in actual combat. I will certainly succeed. I promise to give it up if I cannot gain the recognition of the seniors here."

True energy carried in the Annihilation Team Leader's voice, pressing down like a physical weight.

"This mission is crucial. One of the Eight Great Families. Do you know why I am even listening to your words? It is because you have risen to blue rank. That is all."

The stone fragments on the plain began to bounce. The hem of the Annihilation Team Leader's black robe did not move. Power control beyond divine skill.

"Lightning Flash. I thought well of you. A martial artist who adds wit to prodigious talent -- they are rare. I saw the capacity of a Squad Leader in you. Yet you disappoint me."

*He mentioned blue rank. It is a sign of respect. There is a chance.*

"I apologise."

He stood. Made a cupped fist salute. The masters formed a circle.

He drew the Northern Light sword. Not the Desolate Sword -- Northern Light's material was excellent for what he intended.

"Please protect your ears with internal energy."

He raised his inner power. Split the rising true qi in two -- the Lesser Palace point and Labour Palace point at the centre of his palm. Applied power simultaneously. A chilling light brushed the blade.

WHIIISH!

A wave like a bird's cry spread from the steel.

Around ten white-ranked warriors collapsed where they stood, unconscious before they hit the ground. Blue-ranked masters caught their falling juniors with reflexes born of a lifetime's training.

Silence.

"Sound attack," someone breathed.

"How? Such precise true qi..."

"He applied power enhancement of bladed weapons. At that level it is in the realm of chants."

The blue ranks began discussing among themselves. Principle first, admiration second.

The Annihilation Team Leader's black eyes fixed on Yeon-shin.

"It consumes considerable internal energy."

Yeon-shin answered with a bow. He was not wrong. The technique demanded extreme concentration. Even one casting left him fatigued.

"Precise true qi operation. Hard to use suddenly in chaotic battle. Good for opening a fight -- dampening morale." He was already giving advice. "Even so, we cannot send you alone."

"I will attach two more," Ma Jin said. "Bewitching Sword Demon and Blue-Eyed Demon Sword. They have matched movements with the Lightning Flash before."

The Annihilation Team Leader's dark eyes turned one final time to Yeon-shin.

"Show that it was not recklessness. Prove it with martial might. So none will call you rash."

He turned. His dark robe fluttered once and was gone.

Ma Jin led Yeon-shin to the edge of the mountain range.

"That sound attack. Did you create it?"

"Yes."

The scar on Ma Jin's face twitched.

"Why did you not say so earlier?"

"What martial artist reveals all their personal techniques? The person themselves would end up dead, countered far from home. This time I had no choice."

"...You are not wrong." Ma Jin shook his head. "Yeon-shin. You almost died on this mission. Listen. Long-lived martial artists recognise their grudges."

Yeon-shin's eyebrows twitched at the word *long-lived*.

"Your impact on the murim is not ordinary. Word came from the Superintendent's Office. There are those investigating the Lightning Flash's movements. The Tyrant Sword Sect. The Deep Martial Alliance. The Blood Flame Sect."

*Three factions, all sharpening blades for me.*

"It is something every martial artist must handle," Ma Jin continued. "Even so -- you are excessive. It would be hard to find precedent for making so many powerful enemies in such a short time. Especially at your age. Always be vigilant. Remember this."

Concern filled his voice. Yeon-shin nodded.

*I have been running without pause since I entered the murim.*

He was glad his enemies were great sects. The more of their necks he cut, the more merit he accumulated.

He fell into internal energy cultivation as they walked back. Refining true qi operation. Growing stronger with every step, separate from Ma Jin's warning.

The door inside him rattled.

He kept it shut.

---
